plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize the transition from Module 1 innovator mindset work to Module 2 leadership identity work, including why leadership capacity becomes the next required focus.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From Module 1 completion to readiness for leadership identity work through a reflective bridge that reframes opportunity recognition as leadership preparation.
plan_units.rationale: This is the direct subject of the summary slide and bridge narration. It orients learners to completion, transition, and next-module purpose without introducing unsupported Module 2 detail.
source_refs: Module 1 Complete: The Mindset is Set.
In Module 2, we will move from Mindset to Identity.
plan_units.title: Module 1 Completion and Forward Bridge

plan_units.learning_objective: Recall the core Module 1 foundations that learners are carrying forward: macro forces in medicine and the Opportunity Radar as a structured way to identify innovation targets.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From recalling isolated Module 1 concepts to seeing them as a coherent innovation foundation through synthesis of mindset, diagnosis, and opportunity-identification pillars.
plan_units.rationale: The bridge explicitly references prior Module 1 accomplishments and the source pillars define the specific conceptual carry-forwards that should be surfaced in assessment-driven reflection.
source_refs: You have mapped out the macro forces changing medicine
filled your Opportunity Radar with vital targets.
past frustrations, present workarounds, and future trends.
plan_units.title: What Module 1 Built: System Awareness and Opportunity Detection

plan_units.learning_objective: Use the Module 1 knowledge check as a reflection surface to verify retained understanding of key concepts before progressing to leadership identity work.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From recalling isolated Module 1 concepts to seeing them as a coherent innovation foundation through synthesis of mindset, diagnosis, and opportunity-identification pillars.
plan_units.rationale: The comprehensive knowledge check supplies explicit assessment evidence of what learners should retain at this bridge point. Using it as a reflection checkpoint fits the operator framing and remains grounded in source content.
source_refs: creating new value that is successfully adopted
plan_units.title: Assessment Reflection: What the Learner Should Now Know

plan_units.learning_objective: Map identified Opportunity Radar targets and system problems onto the need for leadership traits that enable action inside a complex health system.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From recalling isolated Module 1 concepts to seeing them as a coherent innovation foundation through synthesis of mindset, diagnosis, and opportunity-identification pillars.
plan_units.rationale: This is the core bridge move requested in the framing and supported by the narration: ideas and opportunity targets are insufficient without the leader who can champion them.
source_refs: an idea is only as good as the leader
inspire others to join your cause
plan_units.title: Opportunity Radar to Leadership Readiness Mapping

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain why opportunity recognition is insufficient without political savvy, communication skill, and leadership identity in hospital intrapreneurship.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From confidence in having the right problem to recognition that execution depends on leadership identity through a candid reframing of what actually determines innovation success in health systems.
plan_units.rationale: This is the highest-pedagogical-weight conceptual pivot in the bridge narration and deserves clustering because it carries multiple explanatory beats: idea insufficiency, matrixed-system complexity, and required leadership capacities.
source_refs: But an idea is only as good as the leader
You are no longer just reacting to the system
other 90% is having the political savvy
the communication skills, and the leadership identity
plan_units.title: Why Ideas Alone Fail in Matrixed Health Systems

plan_units.learning_objective: Connect the need for leadership identity to Module 1's intrapreneurial mandate of navigating politics, budgets, and legacy systems inside the hospital.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From confidence in having the right problem to recognition that execution depends on leadership identity through a candid reframing of what actually determines innovation success in health systems.
plan_units.rationale: This deepens the bridge by tying the narration's leadership claim to the explicit Module 1 pillar that innovation occurs within existing institutional constraints.
source_refs: navigating existing politics, budgets, and legacy systems.
plan_units.title: Intrapreneurial Reality: Innovation Happens Inside Constraints

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify the leadership capacities named in the bridge as the execution layer required to move from idea to adoption.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From confidence in having the right problem to recognition that execution depends on leadership identity through a candid reframing of what actually determines innovation success in health systems.
plan_units.rationale: This resolves the cluster by naming the specific capacities the narration elevates and linking them to successful followership and system redesign.
source_refs: define who you are as a leader
plan_units.title: Leadership Traits as the Missing Execution Layer

plan_units.learning_objective: Preview the stated Module 2 focus: defining leadership identity, communicating vision, and inspiring others to join the cause.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From reflection on Module 1 accomplishment to anticipation for Module 2 identity work through a concise preview of the leader-development agenda ahead.
plan_units.rationale: This is the explicit destination of the bridge and should remain a synthesis-style singleton because its value is the simultaneous whole of the closing takeaway.
source_refs: You will begin to define who you are as a leader
how you can inspire others to join your cause.
it is time to build the leader who will solve it.
plan_units.title: Module 2 Preview: Building the Leader Who Solves the Problem

research_goals.pedagogical_intent: Learner may need the original evidence basis behind the assessed macro-force facts such as the 73-day medical knowledge doubling claim and the healthcare expenditure figure to reinforce confidence in the urgency frame.
Learner may benefit from primary-source support for the claim that psychological safety is necessary for surfacing operational failure in healthcare teams.

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Expanded prose synthesis of the Module 1 pillars named or implied in the bridge, including intrapreneurship, kind vs. wicked environments, functional flexibility, diagnosis before solution, symptom vs. root cause, lead domino, 5 Whys, Opportunity Radar, and psychological safety.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: Short bridge-language recap of macro forces and Opportunity Radar.

exercises.prompt_intent: Prompt learners to retrieve the named Module 1 concepts most relevant to later leadership work.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: Falling in Love with the Problem
exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners explain how root-cause thinking differs from solution attachment.

sections.narrative_intent: Provide the depth that lets learners reassemble Module 1 as a coherent operating system rather than a list of disconnected ideas.
sections.title: Module 1 Foundations to Carry Forward

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A read-in-depth reflection worksheet keyed to the comprehensive module knowledge check topics, with space for learners to note confidence, confusion, and implications for future leadership work.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: A short checkpoint prompt that signals assessment-based reflection without reproducing every item.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: Without a shared belief that it is safe to report errors and propose unconventional ideas, teams cannot uncover the actual broken parts of a system.
exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to explain why psychological safety is necessary for system diagnosis.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: To repeatedly question a systemic friction point until the true underlying root cause, or Lead Domino, is identified.
exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to interpret the operational purpose of the 5 Whys.

sections.narrative_intent: Convert the assessment from a terminal check into a bridge reflection tool that makes readiness visible.
sections.title: Knowledge Check Reflection Notes

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A fuller explanation of why idea quality does not guarantee adoption in hospitals, tied to matrixed systems, politics, budgets, legacy workflows, and the distinction between finding a problem and mobilizing a coalition.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The memorable bridge line that idea is only a fraction of the battle.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: The course is about innovating inside the hospital, which requires navigating existing politics, budgets, and legacy systems.
exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners analyze why a strong idea could stall inside a hospital despite clear problem-solution fit.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: the other 90% is having the political savvy, the communication skills, and the leadership identity
exercises.prompt_intent: Prompt learners to connect one Opportunity Radar target to a leadership capability needed for advancement.

sections.narrative_intent: Make explicit that the next developmental task is not more ideation alone but becoming the kind of leader who can earn adoption.
sections.title: Why Leadership Becomes the Next Bottleneck

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A reflective pre-work section asking learners to articulate how they currently define themselves as leaders, where they feel least confident communicating vision, and what kind of cause they want others to join.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The concise preview of identity, vision, and inspiration.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: You will begin to define who you are as a leader
exercises.prompt_intent: Invite learners to draft a first-pass statement of the leader they need to become to solve a chosen clinical problem.

sections.narrative_intent: Turn the closing preview into actionable readiness for the next module without adding unsupported Module 2 content.
sections.title: Module 2 Readiness Prompt

lesson_plan.lesson_summary: A concise assessment-bridge lesson that closes Module 1 by synthesizing its core pillars, uses the knowledge check as a reflection surface, and reframes opportunity identification as insufficient without leadership identity. The sequence moves from completion acknowledgment, to what learners now know, to why execution inside a matrixed health system demands political savvy and communication, and finally to a source-grounded preview of Module 2's leadership-identity focus.
